text:
A rhyming couplet:
She shook her head at the spoiled little brat,

A rhyming couplet:
She struggled to be patient with the little brat,

A rhyming couplet:
He lost his patience with the mischievous brat,

A rhyming couplet:
She adjusted the brim of her straw summer hat,

A rhyming couplet:
She skimmed the stock pot, pouring off the fat,

A rhyming couplet:
He watched the sizzling rashers lose their fat,

A rhyming couplet:
She stirred the melting beeswax in the vat,

A rhyming couplet:
She memorised the lesson she learned from that,

A rhyming couplet:
He bit his tongue beside the tiresome brat,

A rhyming couplet:
She gave the golden retriever a gentle pat,

A rhyming couplet:
He sealed the lid of the fermenting vat,

A rhyming couplet:
He photographed the freshly deposited scat,

A rhyming couplet:
She trained the clever maze-navigating rat,

A rhyming couplet:
She dropped the mango squarely with a splat,

A rhyming couplet:
She folded up the old beachside mat,

A rhyming couplet:
She stretched the canvas on its frame out flat,

A rhyming couplet:
The cave was home to every sleeping bat,

A rhyming couplet:
She embroidered through the afternoon as she sat,

A rhyming couplet:
He sighed as he chased the screaming brat,

A rhyming couplet:
He saved the dripping, prizing all the fat,

A rhyming couplet:
She edited the chapter and chose to omit,

A rhyming couplet:
She peered through the curtain at the tiny slit,

A rhyming couplet:
He copied the cable pattern stitch by knit,

A rhyming couplet:
She wrote and rehearsed the irreverent skit,

A rhyming couplet:
She cast on forty stitches to begin her knit,

A rhyming couplet:
He turned the roast slowly upon the spit,

A rhyming couplet:
She threaded the ribbon through each little slit,

A rhyming couplet:
He reviewed the draft for details to omit,

A rhyming couplet:
He coaxed the border collie to just sit,

A rhyming couplet:
He pushed the letter through the door's thin slit,

A rhyming couplet:
He carried the torch until it was brightly lit,

A rhyming couplet:
She checked the list for anything to omit,

A rhyming couplet:
She excavated through the layer of the pit,

A rhyming couplet:
She watched the beacon tower blaze when lit,

A rhyming couplet:
She laid the equipment out from the kit,

A rhyming couplet:
He practised every morning for the split,

A rhyming couplet:
The composer celebrated every radio hit,

A rhyming couplet:
She measured all the gases it would emit,

A rhyming couplet:
He swung the maul and watched the log split,

A rhyming couplet:
He organised each component in the kit,

A rhyming couplet:
He coaxed the frightened kitten not to stray,

A rhyming couplet:
He whispered all the things he dared not say,

A rhyming couplet:
The florist crafted a spectacular display,

A rhyming couplet:
He stood back to admire the finished display,

A rhyming couplet:
She painted the misty outline of the bay,

A rhyming couplet:
She mapped the overgrown and hidden way,

A rhyming couplet:
She studied the character she would portray,

A rhyming couplet:
He carted every heavy bale of hay,

A rhyming couplet:
She practised all the gestures to portray,

A rhyming couplet:
The wolf's coat faded into silver gray,

A rhyming couplet:
She counted out the coins she had to pay,

A rhyming couplet:
She rewrote the introduction of the essay,

A rhyming couplet:
She sketched the harbour view throughout the day,

A rhyming couplet:
He rehearsed the dialect he had to portray,

A rhyming couplet:
She watched the stalking lioness approach the prey,

A rhyming couplet:
He polished every trophy on display,

A rhyming couplet:
She tended every hen to help her lay,

A rhyming couplet:
He packed his bags but chose to stay,

A rhyming couplet:
The orchestra swelled at the start of the play,

A rhyming couplet:
She watched the waterfall dissolve to spray,

A rhyming couplet:
He campaigned vigorously for the refugee's plight,

A rhyming couplet:
The fungal spores arrived to spread their blight,

A rhyming couplet:
He warned the child about the frost's sharp bite,

A rhyming couplet:
The sparks were fanned until they could ignite,

A rhyming couplet:
He cleared the desk each morning just to write,

A rhyming couplet:
She examined the hive for every dust mite,

A rhyming couplet:
She polished every medal shining bright,

A rhyming couplet:
She embroidered the wings of the dancing sprite,

A rhyming couplet:
The children caught a glimpse of a forest sprite,

A rhyming couplet:
He measured the tower's total height,

A rhyming couplet:
The prism spread the beam into its coloured light,

A rhyming couplet:
She clenched her fists and readied for the fight,

A rhyming couplet:
She balanced the stack of books standing upright,

A rhyming couplet:
She was always gracious and unfailingly polite,

A rhyming couplet:
He tasted the sharp citrus for its bite,

A rhyming couplet:
He studied every crest of every knight,

A rhyming couplet:
She attached the tail to steady the kite,

A rhyming couplet:
He braced the slumping fence post back upright,

A rhyming couplet:
He launched the diamond-shaped red paper kite,

A rhyming couplet:
She smiled and carried on with quiet spite,

A rhyming couplet:
The medic looped the cotton into a sling,

A rhyming couplet:
He braced himself against the salt-wind's sting,

A rhyming couplet:
She scattered the capers to inject some zing,

A rhyming couplet:
He searched the garden for some flowers to bring,

A rhyming couplet:
She composed a ballad honouring the king,

A rhyming couplet:
He hauled the final boxes he would bring,

A rhyming couplet:
She tied the makeshift baby-carrier sling,

A rhyming couplet:
She crushed the chilli in to give it zing,

A rhyming couplet:
He pressed the wet fabric trying not to cling,

A rhyming couplet:
He attached the kite tail to a length of string,

A rhyming couplet:
She polished every dusty and forgotten thing,

A rhyming couplet:
She embroidered the standard of the king,

A rhyming couplet:
He fashioned a support from cloth as a sling,

A rhyming couplet:
She winced beneath the wasp's unexpected sting,

A rhyming couplet:
He added the ginger for a peppery zing,

A rhyming couplet:
She pressed the soaking towel through the wring,

A rhyming couplet:
He carved the symbol of the coming spring,

A rhyming couplet:
He examined every small and broken thing,

A rhyming couplet:
He tracked the swift on each dark blurring wing,

A rhyming couplet:
She mended every feather of the broken wing,

A rhyming couplet:
She practised every morning trying to excel,

A rhyming couplet:
He broke the centuries-long enchanter's spell,

A rhyming couplet:
She wrote the argument that would compel,

A rhyming couplet:
She sprayed the perimeter hoping to repel,

A rhyming couplet:
The captain struggled hard the riot to quell,

A rhyming couplet:
He couldn't find the words for farewell,

A rhyming couplet:
She cleared her throat with so much left to tell,

A rhyming couplet:
The school reviewed its policy to expel,

A rhyming couplet:
He rang the tower's heavy iron bell,

A rhyming couplet:
He opened up the shop with wares to sell,

A rhyming couplet:
He built the stone surround of the old well,

A rhyming couplet:
She climbed the rooftop and let out a yell,

A rhyming couplet:
She broadcast the findings trying to dispel,

A rhyming couplet:
She practised meditation to help quell,

A rhyming couplet:
He rehearsed the words he needed to tell,

A rhyming couplet:
He coated every surface to repel,

A rhyming couplet:
She pressed her ear against the spiral shell,

A rhyming couplet:
He identified the creature from its shell,

A rhyming couplet:
She practised moving on and not to dwell,

A rhyming couplet:
He braced the deck against the ocean swell,

A rhyming couplet:
He dreaded what disaster might befall,

A rhyming couplet:
She filmed the moment he began to crawl,

A rhyming couplet:
She decorated the atrium of the mall,

A rhyming couplet:
She walked the full echoing length of the hall,

A rhyming couplet:
She knitted the borders of the shawl,

A rhyming couplet:
He photographed the leaf's slow spiral fall,

A rhyming couplet:
He mapped the limits of the endless sprawl,

A rhyming couplet:
He answered every single urgent call,

A rhyming couplet:
He watched the rugby forwards form the maul,

A rhyming couplet:
She had the nerve, the cheek, the sheer gall,

A rhyming couplet:
He watched the infant practice how to crawl,

A rhyming couplet:
She encouraged the toddler to crawl,

A rhyming couplet:
He stepped between the fighters in the brawl,

A rhyming couplet:
He loaded the van for the long haul,

A rhyming couplet:
She strained to recall the faded memory,

A rhyming couplet:
He painted every corner of the crumbling wall,

A rhyming couplet:
He braced the fence to stop it as it would fall,

A rhyming couplet:
He searched the faded photograph to recall,

A rhyming couplet:
She wrapped the newborn tightly in the shawl,

A rhyming couplet:
She watched the ash descend like a grey pall,

A rhyming couplet:
He listed every measure he must take,

A rhyming couplet:
She swept the pathway clear with her bamboo rake,

A rhyming couplet:
She kayaked to the island in the mountain lake,

A rhyming couplet:
The morning mist dissolved above the lake,

A rhyming couplet:
He plunged from the dock into the cold lake,

A rhyming couplet:
She summoned all the courage she could make,

A rhyming couplet:
He stretched his limbs and called a well-earned break,

A rhyming couplet:
He braced himself for the leap it would take,

A rhyming couplet:
The chef seared both sides of the finest steak,

A rhyming couplet:
She splashed cold water on her face to wake,

A rhyming couplet:
She invited every guest to freely partake,

A rhyming couplet:
The baker layered icing on the cake,

A rhyming couplet:
She pressed the tent peg deeply like a stake,

A rhyming couplet:
He acknowledged everything that was at stake,

A rhyming couplet:
She guided the children past the basking snake,

A rhyming couplet:
She glazed the edges of the sirloin steak,

A rhyming couplet:
He explained the origin of the mistake,

A rhyming couplet:
He tidied every border with a rake,

A rhyming couplet:
He greased the tin and set the loaf to bake,

A rhyming couplet:
She poured the tea and took a needed break,

A rhyming couplet:
He swirled and sipped the Rhineland hock,

A rhyming couplet:
He seasoned the overnight pot in the crock,

A rhyming couplet:
She paused and listened for another knock,

A rhyming couplet:
She stored the equipment crates along the dock,

A rhyming couplet:
She simmered the bone broth in the crock,

A rhyming couplet:
He waited long before the second knock,

A rhyming couplet:
He stuffed the stocking up like a fat sock,

A rhyming couplet:
She checked the shelves and replenished the stock,

A rhyming couplet:
He oiled the mechanism of the lock,

A rhyming couplet:
She wove the strands of colour to interlock,

A rhyming couplet:
She rattled every bolt and sticky lock,

A rhyming couplet:
He organised the schedule for every jock,

A rhyming couplet:
The tall ship moored beside the timber dock,

A rhyming couplet:
She arranged the puzzle pieces so they'd interlock,

A rhyming couplet:
He uncorked the bottle of chilled white hock,

A rhyming couplet:
She polished every bollard on the dock,

A rhyming couplet:
He repaired the chime mechanism of the clock,

A rhyming couplet:
He called for help after the electric shock,

A rhyming couplet:
She engineered the sections to interlock,

A rhyming couplet:
He sheltered from the gale behind a rock,

A rhyming couplet:
He studied every heraldic mark on the crown,

A rhyming couplet:
She dyed the fabric to a tawny brown,

A rhyming couplet:
She repaired the enamel on the crown,

A rhyming couplet:
She added the final beads onto the gown,

A rhyming couplet:
He pulled the shutters on the cold street down,

A rhyming couplet:
He showed the failed attempt behind his frown,

A rhyming couplet:
She smoothed the brocade bodice of the gown,

A rhyming couplet:
He dragged the anchor cable grinding down,

A rhyming couplet:
He measured the length of the bridal gown,

A rhyming couplet:
He furrowed both his brows into a frown,

A rhyming couplet:
He reached the shallows only just not drown,

A rhyming couplet:
The dressmaker stitched a lavender ball-gown,

A rhyming couplet:
She authored the text that established her renown,

A rhyming couplet:
He rehearsed his pratfalls dressed up as a clown,

A rhyming couplet:
She grabbed the lifeguard's arm about to drown,

A rhyming couplet:
He draped the chiffon panel of the gown,

A rhyming couplet:
He lit the festival lanterns of the town,

A rhyming couplet:
He gilded every spike upon the crown,

A rhyming couplet:
He tracked the water table sinking down,

A rhyming couplet:
He called for help in danger as he'd drown,
